components.title: Frame (Fixed Size)
components.summary: Proposes a fixed width and/or height to the modified view, positioning it within the resulting frame by an alignment.

properties.name: width
properties.notes: Range 0...500 for the showroom. Pass nil to leave the dimension flexible; expose a 'set width' toggle to allow nil.

properties.name: height
properties.notes: Range 0...500. nil leaves height driven by content.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Positions the content within the frame when the frame is larger than the content.

components.higNotes: Avoid hardcoding pixel sizes that fight Dynamic Type. Prefer flexible frames and let content size itself; reserve fixed frames for genuinely fixed-dimension elements like avatars or icons.

components.title: Frame (Flexible / Min-Ideal-Max)
components.summary: Constrains a view between minimum, ideal, and maximum bounds; .infinity makes the view expand to fill available space.

properties.name: minWidth
properties.notes: Range 0...400. nil leaves the lower bound unconstrained.

properties.name: maxWidth
properties.notes: Use .infinity to expand to fill the parent's proposed width.

properties.name: maxHeight
properties.notes: Use .infinity to fill vertically.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Positions content inside the resolved frame.

components.higNotes: maxWidth: .infinity is the idiomatic way to make buttons and rows span the full width. Pair with alignment to control where content sits inside the expanded frame.

components.title: Padding
components.summary: Adds space around the view along the chosen edges; with no arguments uses a system-default amount that adapts to context.

properties.name: edges
properties.notes: Which edges receive padding.

properties.name: length
properties.notes: Range 0...64. Pass nil to use the system default adaptive padding.

components.higNotes: Prefer system default padding (no length argument) for standard layouts; it adapts across platforms and size classes. Reserve explicit values for design-system spacing tokens.

components.title: Offset
components.summary: Shifts the view's rendered position by a CGSize without affecting its layout footprint (siblings see the original frame).

properties.name: x
properties.notes: Range -100...100. Positive moves right.

properties.name: y
properties.notes: Range -100...100. Positive moves down.

components.higNotes: Offset is purely visual and does not change layout; use it for nudges and animations, not for structural positioning. For layout-affecting moves, use padding or a stack.

components.title: Position
components.summary: Centers the view at an absolute point in the parent's coordinate space, then claims all available space for layout.

properties.name: x
properties.notes: Range 0...300. X coordinate of the view's center in parent space.

properties.name: y
properties.notes: Range 0...300. Y coordinate of the view's center.

components.higNotes: position consumes all proposed space and centers the child at the given point. Use sparingly; stacks and alignment guides are usually clearer for structured layout.

components.title: Fixed Size
components.summary: Fixes the view at its ideal size on the chosen axes, overriding the parent's proposed size (e.g. prevents Text truncation).

properties.name: horizontal
properties.notes: When true, the view keeps its ideal width regardless of the parent proposal.

properties.name: vertical
properties.notes: When true, the view keeps its ideal height.

components.higNotes: The most common use is .fixedSize(horizontal: false, vertical: true) to let multi-line Text grow vertically instead of truncating. Avoid fixing both axes unless the content is genuinely intrinsic.
components.codeTemplate:
Text("A very long line of text that would otherwise truncate")
    .fixedSize(horizontal: {horizontal}, vertical: {vertical})

components.title: Layout Priority
components.summary: Sets the relative priority a view has when a stack divides limited space among its children.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: Range -10...10. Higher values get space first; default for all views is 0.

components.higNotes: Raise layoutPriority on the element that should win when an HStack/VStack is tight (e.g. a title over a trailing badge). Keep the scale small and intentional.

components.title: Alignment Guide
components.summary: Overrides how a view reports its position for a named alignment guide, enabling custom cross-stack alignment.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: The named guide to override. Horizontal guides for HStack cross-alignment, vertical for VStack.

properties.name: offset
properties.notes: Range -50...50. Demonstrates returning d[guide] + offset from computeValue.

components.higNotes: An advanced layout tool. Reach for it only when standard stack alignments and alignment IDs cannot express the relationship. Document the guide math because it is non-obvious to future readers.

components.title: Safe Area Inset
components.summary: Insets the view's safe area by an auxiliary view pinned to an edge (e.g. a floating bar over a scroll view).

properties.name: edge
properties.notes: Edge the auxiliary content pins to. Vertical edges (top/bottom) and horizontal edges (leading/trailing) use different overloads.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Alignment of the inset content along the cross axis.

properties.name: spacing
properties.notes: Range 0...32. Gap between the primary content and the inset view; nil uses the default.

components.higNotes: Use this to keep content clear of floating controls (toolbars, action bars). Scrollable content correctly insets so the last items remain reachable above the bar.

components.title: Safe Area Padding
components.summary: Extends the safe area by a fixed amount on the chosen edges without inserting an auxiliary view.

properties.name: edges
properties.notes: Edges to pad within the safe area.

properties.name: length
properties.notes: Range 0...48. nil uses the system default padding.

components.higNotes: Prefer over manual padding when you want scroll content to respect added margins while still scrolling edge-to-edge. Common for horizontally scrolling carousels.

components.title: Container Relative Frame
components.summary: Sizes a view relative to its nearest container (scroll view, window) rather than its immediate parent, optionally dividing into spaced columns.

properties.name: axes
properties.notes: Which axes are sized relative to the container.

properties.name: count
properties.notes: Range 1...5. Divides the container into N columns/rows (count:span:spacing: overload).

properties.name: span
properties.notes: Range 1...5. How many of the N divisions this view spans.

properties.name: spacing
properties.notes: Range 0...32. Gap between divisions when count > 1.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Alignment of content within the resolved frame.

components.higNotes: The modern way to build snapping carousels: combine with .scrollTargetBehavior(.viewAligned). Use count/span to show fractional peeks of the next card.

components.title: Ignores Safe Area
components.summary: Lets the view extend under the safe area (notch, home indicator, keyboard) on the specified regions and edges.

properties.name: regions
properties.notes: Which safe-area regions to ignore. .keyboard prevents content shifting when the keyboard appears.

properties.name: edges
properties.notes: Edges on which to extend beyond the safe area.

components.higNotes: Reserve for full-bleed backgrounds and media. Never place interactive controls or critical text in the ignored region — keep them within the safe area.

components.title: Foreground Style
components.summary: Sets the style (color, hierarchical level, gradient, or material) used to fill foreground content such as text and symbols.

properties.name: primary
properties.notes: Primary fill style. Hierarchical styles (.secondary etc.) adapt to context. Supports up to three layered styles via the multi-argument overload.

properties.name: color
properties.notes: Live color when a solid Color is chosen for primary.

components.higNotes: Prefer hierarchical styles (.secondary/.tertiary) and semantic colors over raw .white/.black so content adapts to Dark Mode and vibrancy. Supersedes the deprecated foregroundColor.

components.title: Background
components.summary: Layers a style or view behind the modified view, optionally clipped to a shape; can also extend into ignored safe-area edges.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: Background fill. Materials provide system blur; provide an opaque fallback for Reduce Transparency.

properties.name: shape
properties.notes: Optional clip shape for the background fill (the in: parameter).

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Alignment of a background view built with the @ViewBuilder overload.

components.higNotes: Use materials for layered, translucent surfaces; always supply an opaque fallback under Reduce Transparency. Avoid raw .white/.black backgrounds — use semantic .background or design tokens.

components.title: Overlay
components.summary: Layers a style or view in front of the modified view, sized to match it, aligned by the given alignment.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: Overlay fill when using the style+shape overload (e.g. a scrim or stroke gradient).

properties.name: shape
properties.notes: Clip shape for the style overlay.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Alignment for a @ViewBuilder overlay, e.g. a badge in the corner.

components.higNotes: Ideal for badges, strokes, and scrims that must track the host view's size. Keep overlays non-interactive unless intended; combine with allowsHitTesting(false) for decorative layers.

components.title: Border
components.summary: Draws a rectangular border of the given style and width along the view's edges.

properties.name: content
properties.notes: Border style — typically a Color, but any ShapeStyle works.

properties.name: width
properties.notes: Range 0...12. Border line width.

components.higNotes: border draws a square-cornered stroke that ignores clip shapes. For rounded borders use .overlay(RoundedRectangle().stroke(...)) instead.

components.title: Clip Shape
components.summary: Clips the view to the outline of any Shape, hiding anything outside the shape's path.

properties.name: shape
properties.notes: The clipping shape. Use .rect(cornerRadii:) for per-corner control.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: Anti-aliasing / even-odd fill rule. Default is fine for most cases.

components.higNotes: Preferred replacement for the deprecated cornerRadius modifier. Apply before shadow so the shadow follows the clipped silhouette.

components.title: Clipped
components.summary: Clips the view to its own bounding rectangle, hiding content that overflows the frame.

properties.name: antialiased
properties.notes: Smooths the clip edge at a small performance cost.

components.higNotes: Use to prevent oversized children (e.g. a scaled image) from spilling past their frame. For non-rectangular clips, use clipShape.

components.title: Corner Radius (Deprecated)
components.summary: Clips the view to a rounded rectangle of the given radius. Deprecated as of OS 26.5 — use clipShape or fill instead.

properties.name: radius
properties.notes: Range 0...48. Corner radius applied uniformly to all four corners.

properties.name: antialiased
properties.notes: Smooths the rounded edges.

components.higNotes: Deprecated in OS 26.5. Migrate to .clipShape(.rect(cornerRadius:)) or .clipShape(RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius:)). Include in the showroom only as a labeled legacy/deprecated example.

components.title: Mask
components.summary: Uses the alpha channel of a mask view to control which parts of the modified view are visible.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Alignment of the mask content relative to the masked view.

properties.name: maskShape
properties.notes: The mask content; opaque areas show the view, transparent areas hide it. Gradient masks create fades.

components.higNotes: Great for gradient fade-outs at scroll edges and shaped reveals. Heavy masks can be expensive; pair with drawingGroup() if animating.

components.title: Shadow
components.summary: Draws a drop shadow behind the view with configurable color, blur radius, and offset.

properties.name: color
properties.notes: Shadow color including its opacity.

properties.name: radius
properties.notes: Range 0...40. Blur radius of the shadow.

properties.name: x
properties.notes: Range -20...20. Horizontal offset.

properties.name: y
properties.notes: Range -20...20. Vertical offset.

components.higNotes: Use subtle shadows for elevation; keep them consistent via design tokens. Apply after clipShape so the shadow follows the clipped outline. For inner/colored shadows on shapes use .shadow(.inner(...)) ShapeStyle modifiers.
components.codeTemplate:
Text("Elevated")
    .padding()
    .background(.regularMaterial, in: RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 12))
    .shadow(color: {color}, radius: {radius}, x: {x}, y: {y})

components.title: Opacity
components.summary: Sets the transparency of the view and all its content, from fully transparent (0) to fully opaque (1).

properties.name: opacity
properties.notes: Range 0...1. Multiplies the existing opacity of the content.

components.higNotes: Use for fades and disabled-state dimming. Avoid driving opacity to 0 to 'hide' interactive views — they remain hittable; use conditional rendering or allowsHitTesting(false).

components.title: Tint
components.summary: Overrides the accent color for controls within the view hierarchy (buttons, toggles, progress, selection).

properties.name: tint
properties.notes: Tint applied to controls. Some overloads accept any ShapeStyle. nil reverts to the inherited accent.

components.higNotes: Prefer tint over deprecated accentColor. Use sparingly and consistently; the system tint already conveys interactivity, so reserve custom tints for brand or semantic meaning.

components.title: Glass Effect (Liquid Glass)
components.summary: Applies a Liquid Glass material to the view — a dynamic translucent surface that refracts and reflects its surroundings.

properties.name: glass
properties.notes: Glass variant. Chain .tint(_:) and .interactive(_:) for tinted/interactive glass.

properties.name: shape
properties.notes: Shape the glass is clipped to (the in: parameter). Defaults to Capsule when omitted.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Toggles the glass effect on/off without removing the modifier.

components.higNotes: Adopt for floating controls and overlays per iOS 26 design. Group adjacent glass elements in a GlassEffectContainer for correct blending and morphing. Always gate with #available(iOS 26, *) and provide a material fallback.

components.title: Background Extension Effect
components.summary: Extends the view's content beyond the safe area by mirroring and blurring its edges, creating a seamless full-bleed backdrop (iOS 26).

properties.name: enabled
properties.notes: Showroom toggle to apply/remove the modifier and compare against a plain background.

components.higNotes: Designed to pair with edge-to-edge media and Liquid Glass layouts so artwork bleeds gracefully under sidebars and toolbars. Use behind imagery, not behind legible text.

components.title: Rotation Effect (2D)
components.summary: Rotates the view in 2D by an angle around an anchor point, without affecting layout.

properties.name: angle
properties.notes: Range -360...360 degrees. Positive is clockwise.

properties.name: anchor
properties.notes: Point the rotation pivots around.

components.higNotes: Layout footprint stays at the unrotated bounds. Combine with animation for spinners; guard looping rotations with accessibilityReduceMotion.

components.title: Scale Effect
components.summary: Scales the view by independent x/y factors around an anchor, without affecting its layout footprint.

properties.name: scaleX
properties.notes: Range 0...3. Horizontal scale factor. Use the single-CGFloat overload for uniform scaling.

properties.name: scaleY
properties.notes: Range 0...3. Vertical scale factor.

properties.name: anchor
properties.notes: Point that stays fixed while scaling.

components.higNotes: Use for press feedback and emphasis. Scaling raster images blurs them; scale vector content where possible. Layout footprint remains unscaled.

components.title: Rotation 3D Effect
components.summary: Rotates the view in 3D around an arbitrary axis with optional perspective foreshortening.
components.minOS: iOS 13.0 / macOS 10.15 / tvOS 13.0 / watchOS 6.0 (perspective variant deprecated on visionOS)

properties.name: angle
properties.notes: Range -180...180 degrees.

properties.name: axis
properties.notes: Rotation axis. (0,1,0) flips horizontally like a card.

properties.name: anchor
properties.notes: Anchor point of the rotation in the view plane.

properties.name: anchorZ
properties.notes: Range -100...100. Z-depth of the anchor.

properties.name: perspective
properties.notes: Range 0...2. Strength of perspective foreshortening. Deprecated on visionOS; use the no-perspective overload there.

components.higNotes: Use for card flips and depth transitions. On visionOS prefer rotation3DEffect without perspective. Guard continuous 3D animation with accessibilityReduceMotion.
components.codeTemplate:
CardFront()
    .rotation3DEffect({angle}, axis: (x: {axis.x}, y: {axis.y}, z: {axis.z}), anchor: .{anchor}, anchorZ: {anchorZ}, perspective: {perspective})

components.title: Projection Effect
components.summary: Applies an arbitrary 3D ProjectionTransform (CATransform3D-style matrix) to the view.

properties.name: transform
properties.notes: The 3x3 projection matrix to apply. For common cases prefer rotationEffect/scaleEffect.

components.higNotes: Low-level; reach for the named transform modifiers first. Use projectionEffect only for custom matrices (skews, shears) not expressible otherwise.

components.title: Transform Effect (2D Affine)
components.summary: Applies a 2D affine transform (translate, scale, rotate, skew) to the view's rendering.

properties.name: transform
options: CGAffineTransform(a: 1, b: 0, c: 0.3, d: 1, tx: 0, ty: 0)
properties.notes: The affine matrix. The last option demonstrates a horizontal skew.

components.higNotes: Useful for skews and combined affine transforms. Does not affect layout. For simple rotate/scale, prefer the dedicated modifiers for clarity and animatability.

components.title: Blur
components.summary: Applies a Gaussian blur to the view's rendered content.

properties.name: radius
properties.notes: Range 0...30. Larger radius blurs more.

properties.name: opaque
properties.notes: When true, treats edge pixels as opaque, avoiding transparent fringing.

components.higNotes: Blur is GPU-bound; avoid animating large blurred areas every frame. For frosted backgrounds prefer Materials over manual blur, which adapt to vibrancy and Reduce Transparency.

components.title: Brightness
components.summary: Adds a constant to each color channel, lightening (positive) or darkening (negative) the view.

properties.name: amount
properties.notes: Range -1...1. 0 leaves the view unchanged; 1 forces white, -1 forces black.

components.higNotes: A simple additive adjustment, not perceptual. For press states prefer opacity or overlay scrims, which read more naturally than raw brightness shifts.

components.title: Contrast
components.summary: Multiplies the color separation from gray, increasing (>1) or decreasing (<1) contrast.

properties.name: amount
properties.notes: Range 0...3. 1 is unchanged; 0 collapses to gray; negative inverts.

components.higNotes: Distinct from the system Increase Contrast accessibility setting (read via accessibilityContrast). Use this modifier only for deliberate visual effects, not to satisfy contrast requirements.

components.title: Saturation
components.summary: Adjusts color intensity; 0 produces grayscale, values above 1 oversaturate.

properties.name: amount
properties.notes: Range 0...2. 0 is grayscale; 1 is unchanged.

components.higNotes: Useful for de-emphasizing inactive media (e.g. desaturate a disabled thumbnail). Combine with opacity for a clear disabled affordance.

components.title: Grayscale
components.summary: Desaturates the view toward gray by the given amount.

properties.name: amount
properties.notes: Range 0...1. 0 unchanged; 1 fully gray.

components.higNotes: Idiomatic for indicating disabled or inactive imagery. Pair with reduced opacity for a stronger disabled read.

components.title: Color Invert
components.summary: Inverts each color channel of the view's content.

properties.name: enabled
properties.notes: Showroom toggle to apply/remove the inversion for before/after comparison.

components.higNotes: A blunt effect; rarely appropriate for production UI. Do not use to fake Dark Mode — adopt semantic colors and color schemes instead.

components.title: Color Multiply
components.summary: Multiplies the view's colors by a tint color, darkening toward that hue.

properties.name: color
properties.notes: .white leaves content unchanged; darker/colored values tint and darken it.

components.higNotes: Multiply blending only darkens. To tint white artwork toward a brand color, prefer foregroundStyle on template/symbol images for crisper, more predictable results.

components.title: Hue Rotation
components.summary: Shifts every color's hue around the color wheel by the given angle.

properties.name: angle
properties.notes: Range 0...360 degrees. Animatable for rainbow cycling effects.

components.higNotes: Fun for playful, animated accents but disorienting if overused. Guard any continuous hue cycling with accessibilityReduceMotion.

components.title: Blend Mode
components.summary: Sets how the view composites with the content behind it (multiply, screen, overlay, etc.).

properties.name: blendMode
properties.notes: Compositing operation against the backdrop.

components.higNotes: Blend modes composite against whatever is behind the view, so results depend on the backdrop. Wrap interacting layers in compositingGroup() to control the blending scope.

components.title: Compositing Group
components.summary: Renders the view and its children as a single flattened layer before applying effects like opacity, blend mode, or shadow.

properties.name: enabled
properties.notes: Toggle to show how grouping changes how opacity/shadow apply to overlapping children.

components.higNotes: Apply before opacity or shadow when overlapping children would otherwise composite individually (e.g. avoid double-darkened overlaps). Defines the blend scope for blendMode.

components.title: Drawing Group (Metal)
components.summary: Flattens the view into an offscreen Metal-rendered image, improving performance for complex composited or animated effects.

properties.name: opaque
properties.notes: When true the offscreen buffer has no alpha channel.

properties.name: colorMode
properties.notes: Color space used for offscreen rendering.

components.higNotes: A performance tool for heavy effect stacks (many blurs/gradients/shadows). It rasterizes content, so do not wrap text you need crisp at all scales. Measure before and after; it can hurt simple views.

components.title: Disabled
components.summary: Disables user interaction for the view and its controls, dimming them and blocking input.

properties.name: disabled
properties.notes: When true, blocks interaction and applies the disabled appearance. Read inside children via @Environment(\.isEnabled).

components.higNotes: Preferred over manual dimming because it also updates accessibility traits (VoiceOver announces 'dimmed/disabled'). Avoid disabling without explaining why the control is unavailable.

components.title: Allows Hit Testing
components.summary: Controls whether the view participates in hit testing; when false, touches pass through to views behind it.

properties.name: enabled
properties.notes: When false, the view (and children) ignore touches but stay visible and enabled-looking.

components.higNotes: Use for purely decorative overlays so they don't intercept taps. Unlike disabled, it does not dim the view or change accessibility traits.

components.title: Content Shape
components.summary: Defines the hit-testing (and optionally preview/drag) shape of the view, enlarging or restricting its tappable region.

properties.name: kind
properties.notes: Which interaction the shape applies to. Default overload applies to .interaction.

properties.name: shape
properties.notes: The shape used for hit testing. Rectangle over an HStack makes the whole row tappable, including spacers.

components.higNotes: Use .contentShape(Rectangle()) to make an entire row tappable instead of just its opaque content. Also shapes context-menu/drag previews via the kind parameter.

components.title: Z-Index
components.summary: Sets the front-to-back stacking order of the view within its parent layout (higher draws on top).

properties.name: value
properties.notes: Range -10...10. Default is 0; ties resolve in declaration order.

components.higNotes: Affects sibling ordering within a single container (ZStack/overlay). Essential for keeping a view on top during matchedGeometryEffect/transition animations where default order would clip it.

components.title: Aspect Ratio
components.summary: Constrains the view's dimensions to a given width-to-height ratio, fitting or filling the parent context. Pass nil to preserve a resizable view's intrinsic ratio.

properties.name: aspectRatio
properties.notes: Range 0.25...4. Width-to-height ratio. nil keeps the content's own aspect ratio (use a 'set ratio' toggle to expose nil).

properties.name: contentMode
properties.notes: .fit scales to be fully visible inside the frame; .fill scales to cover it (may overflow — pair with clipped()).

components.higNotes: The idiomatic way to keep media at a consistent ratio across layouts. There is also an aspectRatio(_:contentMode:) overload that accepts a CGSize. For images, scaledToFit()/scaledToFill() are convenience shorthands.

components.title: Scaled To Fit / Fill
components.summary: Scales the view to fit inside (or fill) its parent while preserving aspect ratio — convenience shorthands for aspectRatio(nil, contentMode:).

properties.name: mode
properties.notes: Selects scaledToFit() (.fit) vs scaledToFill() (.fill). Both are parameterless modifiers; this control just picks which one the showroom applies.

components.higNotes: scaledToFill() commonly overflows the frame, so combine it with .frame(...) plus .clipped() or .clipShape(...) to crop. scaledToFit() is equivalent to aspectRatio(nil, contentMode: .fit).

components.title: Redacted
components.summary: Applies a redaction (such as a placeholder shimmer) to the view hierarchy, replacing real content with a privacy- or loading-style obscured rendering.

properties.name: reason
properties.notes: .placeholder renders generic shapes (loading skeleton); .privacy obscures sensitive content; [] removes redaction. Read inside children via @Environment(\.redactionReasons).

components.higNotes: The native way to build loading skeletons (.placeholder) and to protect sensitive content while screen sharing (.privacy). Use unredacted() or privacySensitive(_:) to opt subviews in/out. Combine with .redacted on a stub layout for skeleton screens.

components.title: Hidden
components.summary: Hides the view unconditionally while keeping it in the layout — it occupies space and affects sibling positioning but is not drawn and cannot be interacted with.

properties.name: hidden
properties.notes: Showroom toggle to apply/remove .hidden() for before/after comparison. hidden() itself takes no arguments; to conditionally remove a view from layout entirely, use an if statement instead.

components.higNotes: Use to reserve layout space for a view you don't want drawn (e.g. keep an HStack's spacing stable). To omit a view from layout entirely, prefer a conditional `if` over .hidden(). Unlike opacity(0), a hidden view also drops out of hit testing.
